1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Post processing that we can do after a post has already been cooked.
4: # For example, inserting the onebox content, or image sizes/thumbnails.
5: 
6: class CookedPostProcessor
7:   include CookedProcessorMixin
8: 
9:   attr_reader :cooking_options, :doc
10: 
11:   def initialize(post, opts = {})
12:     @dirty = false
13:     @opts = opts
14:     @post = post
15:     @model = post
16:     @previous_cooked = (@post.cooked || "").dup
17:     # NOTE: we re-cook the post here in order to prevent timing issues with edits
18:     # cf. https://meta.discourse.org/t/edit-of-rebaked-post-doesnt-show-in-html-only-in-raw/33815/6
19:     @cooking_options = post.cooking_options || opts[:cooking_options] || {}
20:     @cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
21:     @cooking_options = @cooking_options.symbolize_keys
22:     @should_secure_uploads = @post.should_secure_uploads?
23:     @category_id = @post&.topic&.category_id
24: 
25:     cooked = post.cook(post.raw, @cooking_options)
26:     @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
27:     @has_oneboxes = post.post_analyzer.found_oneboxes?
28:     @size_cache = {}
29: 
30:     @disable_dominant_color = !!opts[:disable_dominant_color]
31:     @omit_nofollow = post.omit_nofollow?
32:   end
33: 
34:   def post_process(new_post: false)
35:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("post_process_#{@post.id}", validity: 10.minutes) do
36:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
37:       update_uploads_secure_status
38:       remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply if new_post
39:       post_process_oneboxes
40:       post_process_images
41:       post_process_videos
42:       add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
43:       post_process_quotes
44:       optimize_urls
45:       remove_user_ids
46:       update_post_image
47:       enforce_nofollow
48:       grant_badges
49:       @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
50:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
51:       nil
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def has_emoji?
56:     (@doc.css("img.emoji") - @doc.css(".quote img")).size > 0
57:   end
58: 
59:   def grant_badges
60:     return if @post.user.blank? || !Guardian.new.can_see?(@post)
61: 
62:     BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstEmoji), @post.user, post_id: @post.id) if has_emoji?
63:     if @has_oneboxes
64:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstOnebox), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
65:     end
66:     if @post.is_reply_by_email?
67:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstReplyByEmail), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   def post_process_quotes
72:     @doc
73:       .css("aside.quote")
74:       .each do |q|
75:         post_number = q["data-post"]
76:         topic_id = q["data-topic"]
77:         if topic_id && post_number
78:           comparer = QuoteComparer.new(topic_id.to_i, post_number.to_i, q.css("blockquote").text)
79: 
80:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-post-not-found").strip if comparer.missing?
81:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-modified").strip if comparer.modified?
82:         end
83:       end
84:   end
85: 
86:   def update_uploads_secure_status
87:     @post.update_uploads_secure_status(source: "post processor")
88:   end
89: 
90:   def remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply
91:     return if !SiteSetting.remove_full_quote
92:     return if @post.post_number == 1
93:     return if @doc.xpath("aside[contains(@class, 'quote')]").size != 1
94: 
95:     previous =
96:       Post
97:         .where(
98:           "post_number < ? AND topic_id = ? AND post_type = ? AND NOT hidden",
99:           @post.post_number,
100:           @post.topic_id,
101:           Post.types[:regular],
102:         )
103:         .order("post_number DESC")
104:         .limit(1)
105:         .pluck(:cooked)
106:         .first
107: 
108:     return if previous.blank?
109: 
110:     previous_text = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(previous).text.strip
111:     quoted_text = @doc.css("aside.quote:first-child blockquote").first&.text&.strip || ""
112: 
113:     return if previous_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1') != quoted_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1')
114: 
115:     quote_regexp = %r{\A\s*\[quote.+\[/quote\]}im
116:     quoteless_raw = @post.raw.sub(quote_regexp, "").strip
117: 
118:     return if @post.raw.strip == quoteless_raw
119: 
120:     PostRevisor.new(@post).revise!(
121:       Discourse.system_user,
122:       { raw: quoteless_raw, edit_reason: I18n.t(:removed_direct_reply_full_quotes) },
123:       skip_validations: true,
124:       bypass_bump: true,
125:     )
126:   end
127: 
128:   def extract_images_for_post
129:     # all images with a src attribute
130:     @doc.css("img[src]") -
131:       # minus emojis
132:       @doc.css("img.emoji") -
133:       # minus images inside quotes
134:       @doc.css(".quote img") -
135:       # minus onebox site icons
136:       @doc.css("img.site-icon") -
137:       # minus onebox avatars
138:       @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar") - @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar-inline") -
139:       # minus github onebox profile images
140:       @doc.css(".onebox.githubfolder img")
141:   end
142: 
143:   def update_post_image
144:     upload = nil
145:     images = extract_images_for_post
146: 
147:     @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css("[data-thumbnail]")) do |url, path, sha1|
148:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
149:       break if upload
150:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module CookedProcessorMixin
4:   GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/}
5:   LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper"
6:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100
7:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100
8: 
9:   def post_process_oneboxes
10:     limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size
11:     oneboxes = {}
12:     inlineOneboxes = {}
13: 
14:     Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element|
15:       is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS
16:       map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes
17:       skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url]
18: 
19:       if skip_onebox
20:         if is_onebox
21:           element.remove_class("onebox")
22:         else
23:           remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element)
24:         end
25: 
26:         next
27:       end
28: 
29:       limit -= 1
30:       map[url] = true
31: 
32:       if is_onebox
33:         onebox =
34:           Oneboxer.onebox(
35:             url,
36:             invalidate_oneboxes: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes],
37:             user_id: @model&.user_id,
38:             category_id: @category_id,
39:           )
40: 
41:         @has_oneboxes = true if onebox.present?
42:         onebox
43:       else
44:         process_inline_onebox(element)
45:         false
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     PrettyText.sanitize_hotlinked_media(@doc)
50: 
51:     oneboxed_images.each do |img|
52:       next if img["src"].blank?
53: 
54:       parent = img.parent
55: 
56:       if respond_to?(:process_hotlinked_image, true)
57:         still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
58:         next if !still_an_image
59:       end
60: 
61:       # make sure we grab dimensions for oneboxed images
62:       # and wrap in a div
63:       limit_size!(img)
64: 
65:       next if img["class"]&.include?("onebox-avatar")
66: 
67:       parent = parent&.parent if parent&.name == "a"
68:       parent_class = parent && parent["class"]
69:       width = img["width"].to_i
70:       height = img["height"].to_i
71: 
72:       if parent_class&.include?("onebox-body") && width > 0 && height > 0
73:         # special instruction for width == height, assume we are dealing with an avatar
74:         if (img["width"].to_i == img["height"].to_i)
75:           found = false
76:           parent = img
77:           while parent = parent.parent
78:             if parent["class"] && parent["class"].match?(/\b(allowlistedgeneric|discoursetopic)\b/)
79:               found = true
80:               break
81:             end
82:           end
83: 
84:           if found
85:             img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-avatar"
86:             next
87:           end
88:         end
89: 
90:         if width < 64 && height < 64
91:           img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-full-image"
92:         else
93:           img.delete("width")
94:           img.delete("height")
95:           new_parent =
96:             img.add_next_sibling(
97:               "<div class='aspect-image' style='--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};'/>",
98:             )
99:           new_parent.first.add_child(img)
100:         end
101:       elsif (
102:             parent_class&.include?("instagram-images") || parent_class&.include?("tweet-images") ||
103:               parent_class&.include?("scale-images")
104:           ) && width > 0 && height > 0
105:         img.remove_attribute("width")
106:         img.remove_attribute("height")
107:         parent["class"] = "aspect-image-full-size"
108:         parent["style"] = "--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};"
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     if @omit_nofollow || !SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
113:       @doc
114:         .css(".onebox-body a[rel], .onebox a[rel]")
115:         .each do |a|
116:           rel_values = a["rel"].split(" ").map(&:downcase)
117:           rel_values.delete("nofollow")
118:           rel_values.delete("ugc")
119:           if rel_values.blank?
120:             a.remove_attribute("rel")
121:           else
122:             a["rel"] = rel_values.join(" ")
123:           end
124:         end
125:     end
126:   end
127: 
128:   def post_process_images
129:     extract_images.each do |img|
130:       still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
131:       convert_to_link!(img) if still_an_image
132:     end
133:   end
134: 
135:   def extract_images
136:     # all images with a src attribute
137:     @doc.css("img[src], img[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]") -
138:       # minus data images
139:       @doc.css("img[src^='data']") -
140:       # minus emojis
141:       @doc.css("img.emoji")
142:   end
143: 
144:   def limit_size!(img)
145:     # retrieve the size from
146:     #  1) the width/height attributes
147:     #  2) the dimension from the preview (image_sizes)
148:     #  3) the dimension of the original image (HTTP request)
149:     w, h =
150:       get_size_from_attributes(img) || get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes]) ||
151:         get_size(img["src"])
152: 
153:     # limit the size of the thumbnail
154:     img["width"], img["height"] = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
155:   end
156: 
157:   def get_size_from_attributes(img)
158:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
159:     return w, h if w > 0 && h > 0
160:     # if only width or height are specified attempt to scale image
161:     if w > 0 || h > 0
162:       w = w.to_f
163:       h = h.to_f
164: 
165:       return unless original_image_size = get_size(img["src"])
166:       original_width, original_height = original_image_size.map(&:to_f)
167: 
168:       if w > 0
169:         ratio = w / original_width
170:         [w.floor, (original_height * ratio).floor]
171:       else
172:         ratio = h / original_height
173:         [(original_width * ratio).floor, h.floor]
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: 
178:   def get_size_from_image_sizes(src, image_sizes)
179:     return if image_sizes.blank?
180:     image_sizes.each do |image_size|
181:       url, size = image_size[0], image_size[1]
182:       if url && src && url.include?(src) && size && size["width"].to_i > 0 &&
183:            size["height"].to_i > 0
184:         return size["width"], size["height"]
185:       end
186:     end
187:     nil
188:   end
189: 
190:   def add_to_size_cache(url, w, h)
191:     @size_cache[url] = [w, h]
192:   end
193: 
194:   def get_size(url)
195:     return @size_cache[url] if @size_cache.has_key?(url)
196: 
197:     absolute_url = url
198:     absolute_url = Discourse.base_url_no_prefix + absolute_url if absolute_url =~ %r{\A/[^/]}
199: 
200:     return unless absolute_url
201: 
202:     # FastImage fails when there's no scheme
203:     absolute_url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + absolute_url if absolute_url.start_with?("//")
204: 
205:     # we can't direct FastImage to our secure-uploads url because it bounces
206:     # anonymous requests with a 404 error
207:     if url && Upload.secure_uploads_url?(url)
208:       absolute_url =
209:         Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(absolute_url, include_content_disposition: false)
210:     end
211: 
212:     return unless is_valid_image_url?(absolute_url)
213: 
214:     upload = Upload.get_from_url(absolute_url)
215:     if upload && upload.width && upload.width > 0
216:       @size_cache[url] = [upload.width, upload.height]
217:     else
218:       @size_cache[url] = FastImage.size(absolute_url)
219:     end
220:   rescue Zlib::BufError, URI::Error, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError
221:     # FastImage.size raises BufError for some gifs, leave it.
222:   end
223: 
224:   def get_filename(upload, src)
225:     return File.basename(src) unless upload
226:     return upload.original_filename unless upload.original_filename =~ /\Ablob(\.png)?\z/i
227:     I18n.t("upload.pasted_image_filename")
228:   end
229: 
230:   def is_valid_image_url?(url)
231:     uri = URI.parse(url)
232:     %w[http https].include? uri.scheme
233:   rescue URI::Error
234:   end
235: 
236:   def add_large_image_placeholder!(img)
237:     url = img["src"]
238: 
239:     is_hyperlinked = is_a_hyperlink?(img)
240: 
241:     placeholder = create_node("div", "large-image-placeholder")
242:     img.add_next_sibling(placeholder)
243:     placeholder.add_child(img)
244: 
245:     a = create_link_node(nil, url, true)
246:     img.add_next_sibling(a)
247: 
248:     span = create_span_node("url", url)
249:     a.add_child(span)
250:     span.add_previous_sibling(create_icon_node("far-image"))

303:     # Lightweight immutable value object to avoid holding full ActiveRecord objects in memory
304:     # Includes extension and original_filename for FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_upload
305:     # fallback path when URL doesn't match UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX
306:     UploadData = Data.define(:id, :url, :sha1, :extension, :original_filename)
307: 
308:     def add_remote_uploads_to_archive(tar_filename)
309:       if !SiteSetting.include_s3_uploads_in_backups
310:         log "Skipping uploads stored on S3."
311:         return
312:       end
313: 
314:       log "Downloading uploads from S3. This may take a while..."
315: 
316:       @s3_store = FileStore::S3Store.new
317:       @upload_directory = Discourse.store.upload_path
318:       @download_stats = { downloaded: 0, hardlinked: 0, copied: 0 }
319: 
320:       uploads_by_sha1 = group_remote_uploads_by_sha1
321:       total_uploads = uploads_by_sha1.values.sum(&:size)
322:       log "Found #{uploads_by_sha1.size} unique files from #{total_uploads} total uploads"
323: 
324:       uploads_by_sha1.each_value { |upload_group| process_upload_group(upload_group) }
325: 
326:       log "Finished processing uploads: #{@download_stats[:downloaded]} downloaded, #{@download_stats[:hardlinked]} hardlinked, #{@download_stats[:copied]} copied"
327:

339:

341:            @download_stats[:copied] == 0
342:         log "No uploads found on S3. Skipping archiving of uploads stored on S3..."
343:       end
344:     end

346:     def group_remote_uploads_by_sha1
347:       # Filter remote uploads at DB level and pluck only needed fields
348:       # Local uploads have URLs like "/uploads/..." but NOT protocol-relative "//"
349:       upload_tuples =
350:         Upload.where("url NOT LIKE '/%' OR url LIKE '//%'").pluck(
351:           Arel.sql("COALESCE(NULLIF(original_sha1, ''), sha1)"),
352:           :id,
353:           :url,
354:           :sha1,
355:           :extension,
356:           :original_filename,
357:         )
358: 
359:       uploads_by_sha1 = {}
360:       upload_tuples.each do |sha1_key, id, url, sha1, extension, original_filename|
361:         uploads_by_sha1[sha1_key] ||= []
362:         uploads_by_sha1[sha1_key] << UploadData.new(
363:           id:,
364:           url:,
365:           sha1:,
366:           extension:,
367:           original_filename:,
368:         )
369:       end
370:       uploads_by_sha1
371:     end
372: 
373:     def process_upload_group(upload_group)
374:       primary = upload_group.first
375:       primary_filename = upload_path_in_archive(primary)
376: 
377:       return if !download_upload_to_file(primary, primary_filename)
378: 
379:       upload_group
380:         .drop(1)
381:         .each do |duplicate|
382:           duplicate_filename = upload_path_in_archive(duplicate)
383:           primary_filename = create_hardlink(primary_filename, duplicate, duplicate_filename)
384:         end
385:     end
386: 
387:     def upload_path_in_archive(upload_data)
388:       path = @s3_store.get_path_for_upload(upload_data)

395:       increment_and_log_progress(:downloaded)

398:       log "Failed to download file with upload ID #{upload_data.id} from S3", ex

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class UserAvatar < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :user
5:   belongs_to :gravatar_upload, class_name: "Upload"
6:   belongs_to :custom_upload, class_name: "Upload"
7:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
8: 
9:   after_save do
10:     if saved_change_to_custom_upload_id? || saved_change_to_gravatar_upload_id?
11:       upload_ids = [self.custom_upload_id, self.gravatar_upload_id]
12:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash = {
17:     Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID => User.email_hash("info@discourse.org"),
18:   }
19: 
20:   def self.register_custom_user_gravatar_email_hash(user_id, email)
21:     @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] = User.email_hash(email)
22:   end
23: 
24:   def contains_upload?(id)
25:     gravatar_upload_id == id || custom_upload_id == id
26:   end
27: 
28:   def update_gravatar!
29:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("update_gravatar_#{user_id}") do
30:       begin
31:         self.update!(last_gravatar_download_attempt: Time.zone.now)
32: 
33:         max = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
34: 
35:         # The user could be deleted before this executes
36:         return if user.blank? || user.primary_email.blank?
37: 
38:         email_hash = @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] || user.email_hash
39:         gravatar_url =
40:           "https://#{SiteSetting.gravatar_base_url}/avatar/#{email_hash}.png?s=#{max}&d=404&reset_cache=#{SecureRandom.urlsafe_base64(5)}"
41: 
42:         if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
43:           Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Downloading gravatar from #{gravatar_url}")
44:         end
45: 
46:         # follow redirects in case gravatar change rules on us
47:         tempfile =

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
5:   # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
6:   class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
7:     sidekiq_options queue: "low"
8: 
9:     def execute(args)
10:       return if !Discourse.store.external?
11:       return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
12: 
13:       Upload
14:         .includes(:optimized_images)
15:         .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
16:         .find_in_batches do |uploads|
17:           uploads.each do |upload|
18:             begin
19:               Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
20:             rescue => err
21:               Discourse.warn_exception(
22:                 err,
23:                 message: "Failed to update upload access control",
24:                 env: {
25:                   upload_id: upload.id,
26:                   filename: upload.original_filename,
27:                 },
28:               )
29:             end
30:           end
31:         end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 1.hour
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
9: 
10:       # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
11:       Upload
12:         .by_users
13:         .where(
14:           "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
15:         )
16:         .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
17:         .where(url: "")
18:         .find_each(&:destroy!)
19: 
20:       return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
21: 
22:       # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
23:       last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
24:       if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
25:            (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
26:         return
27:       end
28: 
29:       result = Upload.by_users
30:       Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
31: 
32:       # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
33:       # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
34:       # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
35:       # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
36:       # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
37:       result =
38:         result
39:           .where(
40:             "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
41:           )
42:           .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
43:           .where(
44:             "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
45:           )
46:           .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
47:           .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
48:           .with_no_non_post_relations
49: 
50:       result.find_each do |upload|
51:         next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
52:         upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
53:       end
54: 
55:       ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
56: 
57:       self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
58:     end
59: 
60:     def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
61:       Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def last_cleanup
65:       timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
66:       timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
67:     end
68: 
69:     def reset_last_cleanup!
70:       Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
71:     end
72: 
73:     protected
74: 
75:     def last_cleanup_key
76:       "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

53: 
54:         ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
55:           begin
56:             connection.exec(
57:               "CREATE TEMP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}(url text UNIQUE, etag text, PRIMARY KEY(etag, url))",
58:             )
59: 
60:             connection.copy_data("COPY #{tmp_table_name} FROM STDIN CSV") do
61:               for_each_inventory_row do |row|
62:                 key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
63: 
64:                 next if Rails.configuration.multisite && key.exclude?(multisite_prefix)
65:                 next if key.exclude?("#{type}/")
66: 
67:                 url = File.join(Discourse.store.absolute_base_url, key)
68:                 connection.put_copy_data("#{CGI.unescape(url)},#{row[CSV_ETAG_INDEX]}\n")
69:               end
70:             end
71: 
72:             table_name = @model.table_name
73: 
74:             # backfilling etags
75:             connection.async_exec(
76:               "UPDATE #{table_name}
77:               SET etag = #{tmp_table_name}.etag
78:               FROM #{tmp_table_name}
79:               WHERE #{table_name}.etag IS NULL AND
80:                 #{table_name}.url = #{tmp_table_name}.url",
81:             )

83:             uploads = @scope.where("updated_at < ?", inventory_date)

67:       path = get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
68:       url, optimized_image.etag =
69:         store_file(file, path, content_type: content_type, private: secure)
70:       url
71:     end
72: 
73:     # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
74:     #
75:     # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
76:     # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
77:     # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
78:     #
79:     # options
80:     #   - filename
81:     #   - content_type
82:     #   - cache_locally
83:     #   - move_existing
84:     #   - private
85:     #   - existing_external_upload_key
86:     def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
87:       path = path.dup
88: 
89:       filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
90:       # cache file locally when needed
91:       cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
92: 
93:       cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
94:       if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
95:         cache_control =
96:           "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
97:       end
98: 
99:       options = {
100:         cache_control: cache_control,
101:         content_type:
102:           opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
103:       }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
104: 
105:       # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
106:       # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
107:       # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
108:       options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
109: 
110:       path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
111: 
112:       # if this fails, it will throw an exception
113:       if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
114:         original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
115:         options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
116:         path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
117:         delete_file(original_path)
118:       else
119:         path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
120:       end
121: 
122:       # return the upload url and etag
123:       [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
124:     end
125:

127:       # delete the object outright without moving to tombstone,
128:       # not recommended for most use cases
129:       s3_helper.delete_object(path)

## Step 1 — Orientation

This is **Discourse**, a Rails-based open-source discussion platform. Backend code lives in `app/`, `lib/`, `config/`, `db/`, `spec/`; frontend in `frontend/`; plugins in `plugins/`. The model that represents an uploaded file is `Upload` at `app/models/upload.rb`. It is built on the `HasUrl` concern (`app/models/concerns/has_url.rb`), validated by `UploadValidator` (`lib/validators/upload_validator.rb`), and referenced polymorphically through `UploadReference` (`app/models/upload_reference.rb`). Derived records include `OptimizedImage`, `OptimizedVideo`, `TopicThumbnail`, `PostHotlinkedMedia`, and `UserUpload`.

## Step 2 — Upload contract and shared pieces

**`Upload` model** — `app/models/upload.rb:5`

Core exposed attributes: `url`, `sha1`, `original_sha1`, `original_filename`, `filesize`, `extension`, `width`, `height`, `thumbnail_width`, `thumbnail_height`, `secure`, `access_control_post_id`, `dominant_color`, `verification_status`, `retain_hours`.

Included/shared pieces:

- `HasUrl` concern — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` — provides `extract_url`, `extract_sha1`, `get_from_url`, `get_from_urls`. Used by `Upload` and `OptimizedImage`.
- `UploadValidator` — `lib/validators/upload_validator.rb:5` — validates extensions, file sizes, and site-setting-specific rules.
- `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` — polymorphic join table between uploads and targets (posts, users, categories, groups, badges, theme fields, user profiles/avatars, etc.).

Associations on `Upload`:

- `has_many :optimized_images` — `app/models/upload.rb:27`
- `has_many :optimized_videos` / `:optimized_video_uploads` — `app/models/upload.rb:28-29`
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media` — `app/models/upload.rb:26`
- `has_many :upload_references` — `app/models/upload.rb:31`
- `has_many :posts, through: :upload_references` — `app/models/upload.rb:32`
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails` — `app/models/upload.rb:33`
- `has_many :badges` (via `image_upload_id`) — `app/models/upload.rb:34`

Derived records spawned from an upload:

- `OptimizedImage` — `app/models/optimized_image.rb:3` — thumbnails/resized versions.
- `OptimizedVideo` — `app/models/optimized_video.rb:3` — transcoded videos pointing back to a second `Upload`.
- `TopicThumbnail` — `app/models/topic_thumbnail.rb:9` — topic-list thumbnail attempts.
- `PostHotlinkedMedia` — `app/models/post_hotlinked_media.rb:3` — tracks hotlinked media pulled into posts.

## Step 3 — How a file becomes a stored Upload

1. **Controller entry** — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:25` (`create`), delegates to `UploadsController.create_upload` at `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:299`.
2. **`UploadCreator#create_for`** — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59` — the central creation flow:
   - Extracts image info (`lib/upload_creator.rb:65`), optionally converts HEIF/PNG/JPEG/ICO, fixes orientation, crops avatars, optimizes, downsizes (`lib/upload_creator.rb:91-113`).
   - Computes `sha1` / `unique_hash` for secure uploads (`lib/upload_creator.rb:121-123`).
   - Deduplicates by `sha1` unless secure uploads / external-too-big / thumbnail (`lib/upload_creator.rb:132-148`).
   - Builds the `Upload` record (`lib/upload_creator.rb:166-181`), sets dimensions and dominant color (`lib/upload_creator.rb:183-215`).
   - Determines secure status via `UploadSecurity` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:219-224`).
   - Stores the file via `Discourse.store.store_upload` (`lib/upload_creator.rb:258-259`) or moves an existing external upload (`lib/upload_creator.rb:246-263`).
   - Enqueues avatar thumbnail job for avatar uploads (`lib/upload_creator.rb:275-277`).
3. **Storage layer** — `Discourse.store` resolves to:
   - `FileStore::LocalStore` — `lib/file_store/local_store.rb:6`
   - `FileStore::S3Store` — `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:10`
   - Base path logic in `FileStore::BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:124`
4. **Async fan-out after create**:
   - `Jobs::CreateAvatarThumbnails` — `app/jobs/regular/create_avatar_thumbnails.rb:4`
   - `Jobs::ConvertVideo` — `app/jobs/regular/convert_video.rb:3` (triggered by `Upload#enqueue_video_conversion_job` at `app/models/upload.rb:690`)
   - `Jobs::BackfillDominantColors` — `app/jobs/scheduled/backfill_dominant_colors.rb:4`
   - `Jobs::UpdateAnimatedUploads` — `app/jobs/scheduled/update_animated_uploads.rb:4`
   - `Jobs::MigrateUploadScheme` — `app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb:4`
5. **Post processing** — `Jobs::ProcessPost` calls `CookedPostProcessor#post_process` (`lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:34`), which updates secure status, generates optimized images/topic thumbnails, and links post uploads via `HasPostUploadReferences#link_post_uploads` (`app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:6`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius and risk ranking

**Highest risk — would page immediately if broken**

1. **`FileStore::BaseStore` / `LocalStore` / `S3Store`** — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7`, `lib/file_store/local_store.rb:6`, `lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:10`
   - Any change to how `Upload#url` is stored or resolved propagates here. `get_path_for_upload` relies on `url` regex and `sha1/extension/id`.
2. **`UploadCreator`** — `lib/upload_creator.rb:5`
   - All upload creation flows go through here. Changing how `Upload` stores data or derives URLs requires updating creation, deduplication, and secure-status assignment.
3. **`Upload#destroy`** — `app/models/upload.rb:212`
   - Currently calls `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` then `super`. If derived-data teardown changes, `remove_upload` and dependent associations must be coordinated.
4. **`OptimizedImage`** — `app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`
   - Directly composes `Upload`, depends on `upload.sha1`, `upload.id`, `upload.secure?`, and `Discourse.store` paths.
5. **`Post` / `HasPostUploadReferences`** — `app/models/post.rb:6`, `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:3`
   - Linking and security updates depend on `Upload` lookup by URL/sha1.

**High risk**

6. **`Email::Receiver` / `Email::Styles` / `Email::Sender`** — `lib/email/receiver.rb:1373`, `lib/email/styles.rb:361`, `lib/email/sender.rb:364`
   - Attachments and secure-image inlining depend on `Upload.sha1`, `original_sha1`, `url`, and `UploadMarkdown`.
7. **`BackupRestore::Creator` / `UploadsRestorer`** — `lib/backup_restore/creator.rb:126`, `lib/backup_restore/uploads_restorer.rb:57`
   - Backup grouping uses `sha1`/`original_sha1`; restore remaps URLs and rebakes posts.
8. **`UploadsController`** — `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:5`
   - Show/short/secure actions resolve uploads by sha1/id/url.
9. **`CookedPostProcessor` / `CookedProcessorMixin`** — `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb:6`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:3`
   - Image sizing, lightboxes, and secure media rely on `Upload.get_from_url` and store URLs.

**Medium risk**

10. **`UserAvatar` / `UserProfile`** — `app/models/user_avatar.rb:3`, `app/models/user_profile.rb:3`
    - Background/avatar uploads reference `Upload` by ID; teardown must clear these references (already done in `Upload#before_destroy`/`after_destroy`).
11. **`SiteSettingExtension` / `SiteIconManager`** — `lib/site_setting_extension.rb:1331`, `lib/site_icon_manager.rb:121`
    - Site setting uploads and favicons resolve upload IDs to URLs.
12. **`ThemeField`** — `app/models/theme_field.rb:126`
    - Theme uploads and SVG sprites reference uploads.
13. **`Jobs::CleanUpUploads`** — `app/jobs/scheduled/clean_up_uploads.rb:4`
    - Orphan cleanup depends on `UploadReference`, `sha1`, and `url`.

**Re-verify after any contract change**

- Upload creation and duplicate detection.
- Optimized image/video generation and cleanup.
- Secure upload ACL updates and presigned URL serving.
- Post rebaking and `UploadReference` sync.
- Email attachment inlining and secure-image stripping.
- Backup/restore with both local and S3 stores.
- Avatar/profile background upload and replacement.
- Site setting/theme upload URL resolution.
- Chat/AI plugin upload handling.

### Model and shared pieces to edit

- `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` — URL/sha1 extraction and lookup.
